items.title: Who will bear the brunt of proposed US-EU tariff increases? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump said he would slap 30% tariffs on imports from the EU and Mexico starting on August 1st. Weeks of negotiations with Brussels failed to reach a comprehensive trade deal. Trump blamed ''long-term and persistent trade deficits'' with the EU. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen says the EU is prepared to impose countermeasures if necessary, in order to protect its interests. On Mexico, Trump says the government has not done enough to curb cross-border drug trafficking. Trump made similar tariff threats against dozens of other trading partners earlier this week.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 EU prepared to impose countermeasures against US tariffs
0:37 DW Correspondent, Janelle Dumalaon in Washington, on how cost of tariffs will be passed on to consumers
5:40 Financial correspondent Jens Korte and DW Correspondent in Tessa Walther in Brussels on potential transatlantic impact of tariffs

#tariffs #eutariffs #trump 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How was fuel cut off to Air India passenger jet prior to crashing? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A preliminary Air India crash report shows that there was pilot confusion as to whether the plane's two fuel cutoff switches had been flipped, preventing the plane's engines from getting fuel.  

Chapter Breakdown: 
0:00 Preliminary report on Air India crash reveals confusion in cockpit
0:30 Sanjay Lazar, Aviation Expert, CEO of Avialaz Consultants, on how report was leaked to US media prior to being published

items.title: Palestinian-American killed in Israeli settler attack in the West Bank | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A 20-year-old Palestinian American, Sayfollah “Saif” Musallet, was beaten to death in an Israeli settler attack in the occupied West Bank's Sinjil town, says the Palestinian Health Ministry. Hussein al-Shalab, 23, was also killed. Israel's military says it is investigating. Musallet was born in Florida and had traveled to the Israeli-occupied West Bank "to spend time with loved ones," according to a family statement. They are demanding justice and calling on the US to launch an investigation.

#dwnewsshorts #israelisettlers #westbank

items.title: Trump announces 30% tariffs on EU and Mexico to start on August 1st, but will he follow through?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump had announced that he will impose 30 % tariffs on imports from the EU and Mexico starting next month. Weeks of negotiations with Brussels failed to reach a comprehensive trade deal. Trump blamed ''long-term and persistent trade deficits'' with the EU. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen says the EU is prepared to take the necessary steps to safeguard its economic interests. On Mexico, Trump says the government has not done enough to curb cross-border drug trafficking. Trump made similar tariff threats against dozens of other trading partners earlier this week.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Trump announces tariffs on EU and Mexico and threatens higher tariffs if EU and Mexico respond with higher tariffs on US

items.title: Russia floods Ukrainian skies with over 620 missiles and drones in large overnight attack | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine calls for its allies to send an increased supply of drone interceptors to the country as Russia launches another massive overnight assault on Ukraine. Russia sent over 620 drones and missiles to strike cities across Ukraine, including Lviv and Kharkiv. The production costs of drone production and drone interception is shaping the direction of the war for both Russia and Ukraine. North Korea has become a key supplier in long-range missiles to Russia.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Russia's massive overnight strike hits cities across Ukraine
2:06 Domitilla Sagramoso, military analyst at Kings College London, on Ukraine's air defense needs

#russiadrones
#ukrainerussiawar
#ukrainiandefense

items.title: How gold keeps Sudan at war | DW Shorts
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Sudan is more than two years into a brutal war. What many people don't know is that the conflict is also fueled by a scramble for gold. The two main warring parties, the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), have been ramping up gold production throughout the war and are using it to fund their war efforts. Gold is increasingly being smuggled across the border to neighboring countries like Egypt. It's also one of the ways global players such as the United Arab Emirates are entangled in the conflict. Dubai reportedly provides weapons like drones in return for gold.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Trump is 'angry and personally frustrated' that Putin has thwarted him | To The Point
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine is under the heaviest Russian air assaults since the start of the war, Russia has fired hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles, forcing residents of the capital Kyiv and other cities to spend their nights in air raid shelters. Just as Ukraine is facing one of its most difficult moments since the full-scale invasion, the United States has been keeping it guessing. After the Pentagon said last week that it would pause some arms shipments, President Trump is now saying the US WILL be supplying some weapons. What kind of damage has this uncertainty caused? This week on To The Point we’re looking at what it means when “Putin attacks, and Trump dithers: How long can Ukraine hold on?”

Our guests:
Wolfgang Ischinger, President of the Foundation Council, Munich Security Conference
Gustav Gressel, military expert
Melinda Crane, journalist
Nick Connolly, DW Correspondent reporting from Ukraine

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Can Ukraine afford to defend itself against Russian drone strikes? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald  Trump told NBC News that the US will start selling weapons to Ukraine, with NATO allied countries footing the bill. 
Trump did not mention Patriot missile systems specifically but he has recently said Kyiv would need Patriots to defend itself. During the interview Trump also said he would be making a "major announcement about Russia on Monday" but did not disclose further details.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has repeated his call for defense systems and tougher sanctions on Russia after an overnight raid left two people dead. On social media, he said the vast majority of more than 600 Russian drones and missiles were destroyed in the attack, which targeted cities across the country. Russia's current strategy is to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses with large numbers of drones. Ukraine is adapting its tactics as the nighttime onslaughts have become more frequent.

00:00 Russian attacks Ukraine with drones
02:52 DW speaks with Marina Miron from King's College London

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Preliminary report on cause of Air India plane crash released | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A preliminary report into last month's Air India disaster indicates fuel supply to both engines was cut off shortly after takeoff. 260 people were killed in the crash, after the Boeing 787 Dreamliner departed Ahmedabad bound for London. 
According to the report, the cockpit voice recording reveals that one of the pilots can be heard asking the other why he moved the fuel switches to "cut off." The other pilot responded that he did not do so. Setting the fuel switches to "cut off" starves the engines of fuel. DW's Sandra Petermann reports from Delhi.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
